cases.title: Kang et al. v. Korean Central Presbytery
cases.synopsis: Three Teaching Elders complained that Korean Central Presbytery clearly erred in declining to indict a Teaching Elder after a BCO 31-2 investigation into allegations of financial impropriety, spiritual abuse, and physical abuse. The SJC held that KCP did not clearly err, given the age of the allegations and the deference owed to lower courts on factual and discretionary matters; the dissent argued that KCP's investigation was constitutionally deficient for failing to exercise due diligence.

cases.title: Suh v. Korean Southeastern Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE Sam Jung Suh appealed his indefinite suspension by Korean Southeastern Presbytery following a trial on charges of financial misconduct. The SJC reversed the conviction in whole, finding that the pre-investigative and BCO 31-2 investigation phases were marred by grave constitutional errors including failure to comply with BCO 27-5, failure to demand satisfactory explanations from TE Suh, improper expansion of the investigation, and absence of a valid antecedent condition necessary to institute process; multiple dissents argued the SJC should have deferred to the lower court's factual findings and trial verdict.
